metadata.description: Public cross-platform (Claude Code + Codex) plugin marketplace for smorinlabs

plugins.name: explain
plugins.description: Concrete-anchored explanation shorthand — /explain answers in a fixed anatomy: what it is, just-enough context, a real before/after example, and the payoff — with options/deeper/steps modes inferred from the target, and follow-up aware: a bare explain after an explanation diagnoses what's blocking action (step back to the bigger picture, clearer language, sharper example) and rewrites rather than repeats.
plugins.author: Steve Morin

plugins.name: factor-harness
plugins.description: Architecture-aware review, refactor, and cross-implementation dedup — surfaces bugs, smells, and precedent gaps, then emits specs for Superpowers to execute.
plugins.author: Steve Morin

plugins.name: guided-research
plugins.description: Orchestration layer over the built-in /deep-research skill — decides when deep research is worth doing, shapes the prompt with project constraints, invokes the engine, and organizes results into a durable, reusable research tree.
plugins.author: Steve Morin

plugins.name: project-harness
plugins.description: Lightweight PROJECTS.md project management — capture, scope, decompose, triage, and drift-audit projects as auto-triggering skills that hand execution off to Superpowers.
plugins.author: Steve Morin

plugins.name: question-walkthrough
plugins.description: Adaptive one-question-at-a-time decision walkthrough. Takes a pile of open questions or undecided to-dos — mined from the conversation, read from a pointed-at document, given inline, or pulled from task systems — confirms the pile, sequences it by leverage (answers that could moot or reshape other questions go first), then walks it one AskUserQuestion at a time. Every non-obvious question is fronted by a pre-read — why it exists, impact, trade-offs, terms — delivered in a chat turn that ends BEFORE the dialog is raised (same-turn context is invisible context). Answer notes are classified and honored: option modifiers apply immediately; directives — extra work or a redirect that replaces the answer — get a one-line repeat-back and confirm, then run in an end-of-walk batch by default. After every answer the remaining pile is re-planned: mooted questions dropped loudly, order revised, implied follow-ups added with consent. Decisions are recorded back at their source and summarized in a final outcome table with parked items and directive outcomes called out. Pure-markdown skill, portable across Claude Code and Codex.
plugins.author: Steve Morin

plugins.name: reader-steps
plugins.description: Style spec for rendering actions only the reader can or will perform: a delineated task block toward the response's end — bounded frame (blockquote rail plus horizontal rules), a Completes line binding it to the tracked item by exact ID and title, steps numbered with a stable tag and grouped by surface (terminal, browser, desktop/system UI, phone, physical world) under merged dividers, each step titled by its outcome with the literal command or UI path below it and a ✓ verification line, closing with the immediate next move. Scales down to a one-line inline form for a single step and up with a map and stop points past eight; breadcrumb navigation promotes to one hop per line past three hops or on any caveat; reactive actions nest inside the step that triggers them; in-flight processes re-render across turns as a progress scoreboard; errors state cause and fix. Decisions are never steps — they are asked (question-walkthrough) before instructing. Canonical spec behind an always-on digest users keep in global instructions.
plugins.author: Steve Morin

plugins.name: repo-finder
plugins.description: One-command repo resolution for agents: resolve a repo name to every local copy with orientation facts (path, origin, default branch, checkout-vs-worktree kind, branch, dirty state, build tooling) via a config-bounded multi-root scan — falling back to the user's configured GitHub orgs over gh REST-first (GraphQL only as configured fallback) for exact clone names. Deterministic ranking (checkouts, then worktrees labeled with their main repo, then nested copies); recency displayed, not ranked, so the consuming LLM decides. Single-file zero-dependency uv Python script inside the skill; conforms to the CLI Design Standard (small-CLI profile, minimal tier). Replaces token-expensive ls/find cascades and repeated gh repo view identity checks.
plugins.author: Steve Morin

plugins.name: repo-hygiene
plugins.description: Repo & release-readiness checks — CI/pre-commit audit, version drift, README sync, manual test guides, and a PR merge flow that resolves every review thread before merging.
plugins.author: Steve Morin

plugins.name: use-html-theme
plugins.description: Themed-HTML toolkit, two pure skills. use-html-theme: on any HTML-generation request, offer a catalog of visual themes (Birchline warm-editorial, Technical-minimal docs-site, High-contrast-dark dashboard), apply the chosen theme to all subsequent HTML in the session, and optionally persist it per-project — natural language and [theme: x] inline-flag control, side-by-side preview on request. html-codesign: interactive 'pick and export' decision pages — every question opens with a collapsible context-and-recommendation preamble (free-form rich content on a clarity scaffold), the reader answers, skips, or raises a question on each section, folds answered sections to dense summary rows for review, then exports the decision as Markdown/JSON (slim by default, full ADR-style by toggle) with stable IDs for diffable iteration. Portable across Claude Code and Codex.
plugins.author: Steve Morin
